answer.text:
The question contains a common confusion I should flag — let me also pull the Article 47B file to address both possibilities.The question conflates two distinct Articles. Let me address both — but flag the confusion first.

---

## A naming warning first

**Article 47B does not set out a "procedure to vary a trust"** — it is the **interpretation provision** for the Articles 47C–47H **mistake-based set-aside** regime, defining what counts as a qualifying "mistake" (effect, consequences including tax, expected advantage, fact existing at the time). Cite the sub-Article carefully: in formal work, **Article 47** is the variation power; **Articles 47B–H** are the mistake-rescission regime. ([`knowledge/jersey/trusts/article-47-set-aside.md`](knowledge/jersey/trusts/article-47-set-aside.md))

So there are two questions you might mean. Here are both.

## (1) If you want to **vary** trust terms — that is **Article 47 simpliciter** (not 47B)

Article 47 lets the Royal Court approve an arrangement **varying or revoking** trust terms on behalf of those who cannot consent for themselves — **minors**, **interdicts**, **unascertained persons**, and **unborn beneficiaries** — provided the arrangement appears **beneficial** to the person on whose behalf approval is sought. It can also **confer powers on trustees** for transactions that are expedient but lack express authority in the deed.

**Procedure** is by **Representation** in the Royal Court (Samedi Division), supported by:

- the trust deed and amendments;
- the **proposed deed of variation** drafted ready for execution;
- **affidavit evidence** explaining the rationale and proposed benefit;
- **professional cross-border tax/accounting analysis** of the consequences;
- written **adult-beneficiary consents**;
- **guardian ad litem** representation for minors/unborns — typically a senior Jersey Advocate appointed by the Court;
- evidence of **service** on interested parties.

The "beneficial" test is **substantive** — a variation that only benefits consenting adults at the expense of unborns will be refused; actuarial/tax modelling is often required. Article 47 is the Court-led route where the deed's own variation power (Article 37) is exhausted or where there are non-consenting interests. ([`knowledge/jersey/trusts/court-variation.md`](knowledge/jersey/trusts/court-variation.md), `last_verified: 2026-05-15`)

## (2) If you actually meant the **Article 47B–H mistake set-aside** (e.g., undoing a transfer into trust or a power-exercise made under mistake)

- **Article 47E** — set-aside of a **transfer of property to a trust** made under mistake;
- **Article 47G** — set-aside of an **exercise of a fiduciary power** made under mistake.

The Royal Court applies a **three-limb test** (per *In re S & T Trusts* [2015] JRC259): (1) there was a mistake within the wide Article 47B definition; (2) **but for** the mistake the transaction/exercise would not have been made in that form; (3) the mistake was of so serious a character that it would be **just** to grant relief. Jersey deliberately **diverges from** *Pitt v Holt* [2013] UKSC 26 — the English distinction between "mistake" and "mere causative ignorance / inadequate deliberation" does not apply; the Jersey regime is wider.

**Procedure** is by **Representation** in the Royal Court, Samedi Division, supported by affidavit evidence of state of mind, **contemporaneous documentary evidence** (file notes, advice, e-mail — reconstruction affidavits years later are weak), and parallel cross-border tax analysis. ([`knowledge/jersey/trusts/article-47-set-aside.md`](knowledge/jersey/trusts/article-47-set-aside.md), `last_verified: 2026-05-14`)

## Caveats

- Both cited files are **`status: draft`** — the substance was drafted but is unverified against primary source. Verify against the consolidated Trusts (Jersey) Law 1984 at jerseylaw.je before acting.
- If your underlying goal is something else — variation under the deed's own power (Article 37), redirection of a failed purpose trust (Article 47A), or directions to the trustee rather than rescission (Article 51) — the right door is different again. Tell me which you mean and I'll pull the specific procedure.

## Files cited

1. [`knowledge/jersey/trusts/court-variation.md`](knowledge/jersey/trusts/court-variation.md) — Article 47 variation procedure
2. [`knowledge/jersey/trusts/article-47-set-aside.md`](knowledge/jersey/trusts/article-47-set-aside.md) — Articles 47B–47H mistake set-aside regime
